content.problemStatement:
You are given a string (S)(S)(S). Your task is to determine if the string can be a palindrome after deleting at most one character from it.


Input Format

The first line contains one integer nnn, the size of the string                                                                                                               - Next line contains string SSS.


Output Format

Print whether SSS can be made palindrome after deleting at most one character.
constraints: S contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer n (the length of the string) and the second line contains the string S consisting of n lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "true" if the string can become a palindrome after deleting at most one character, otherwise output "false".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Deleting either 'b' (result "aca") or 'c' (result "aba") yields a palindrome.
No single deletion can turn "batr" into a palindrome.
A single character is already a palindrome.

solution.approach: Use two pointers, left starting at 0 and right at n‑1. While the characters match, move both pointers inward. On the first mismatch we have two possibilities: skip the left character or skip the right character. Check if the remaining substring is a palindrome in either case using a simple linear scan. If any check succeeds, the answer is true; otherwise false. This works because at most one deletion is allowed, so the first mismatch determines the only possible deletions.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, where n is the length of the string. Overall O(T·n).
